expectations.text: HANDBOOK.md exists in outputs/ and has at least 1000 characters of content
expectations.evidence: File exists at with_skill/outputs/HANDBOOK.md with 33,596 characters

expectations.text: The handbook has an Overview or introduction section that describes the system's purpose
expectations.evidence: Has '## Overview' as the first section heading, with system purpose description

expectations.text: The handbook mentions both execute_scout and process_turn (the two main MCP tool entrypoints)
expectations.evidence: grep counts: execute_scout=3 mentions, process_turn=6 mentions. Both appear in the MCP Tools (Entry Points) table under At a Glance

expectations.text: The handbook contains at least one table with symptom/cause/recovery columns (or equivalent failure mode documentation)
expectations.evidence: 9 matches for 'symptom/failure mode/Recovery' keywords. Agent summary confirms every runbook section has at least one failure mode as required by skill Phase 4

expectations.text: The handbook includes a numbered verification or smoke test procedure an operator can actually run
expectations.evidence: 16 matches for verification/verify/smoke test. Agent Phase 4 summary confirms 'Verification procedure traces through correctly: pytest runs from source package, ruff lint runs, server startup check works, footgun tests are concrete and runnable'

expectations.text: audit_report.md exists in outputs/ and has at least 500 characters of content
expectations.evidence: File exists with 9,123 characters

expectations.text: Report contains all four standard categories: Accuracy Issues, Coverage Gaps, Currency Concerns, and Structural Issues (or equivalent headings)
expectations.evidence: Report has headings: '### Accuracy Issues' (5 matches), '### Coverage Gaps' (4 matches), '### Currency Concerns' (4 matches), '### Structural Issues' (3 matches) — exact 4-category format from skill

expectations.text: Report explicitly identifies the approval-policy default change (on-failure to on-request) as an accuracy issue or currency concern
expectations.evidence: Accuracy Issue #1: 'Approval-policy default ("coupled to sandbox") does not reflect the on-failure → on-request change from fa1531b — the concrete values are never for read-only, on-request for workspace-write/danger-full-access'

expectations.text: The report is findings-only — it describes issues rather than applying fixes. The report does not claim to have modified the handbook.
expectations.evidence: Report is formatted as an audit report with issue descriptions and recommendations. No claim of having modified the original HANDBOOK.md.

expectations.text: Report ends with or contains a Recommended Priority or priority ranking section
expectations.evidence: '### Recommended Priority' section with 5 numbered items, each explaining why it is prioritized. Located at the end of the report.

expectations.text: HANDBOOK.md exists in outputs/ and has substantial content (at least 5000 characters)
expectations.evidence: File exists with 45,631 characters — full handbook content preserved

expectations.text: The output HANDBOOK.md contains 'on-request' as the approval-policy default value
expectations.evidence: 1 match for 'on-request'. Agent confirms: updated from 'coupled to sandbox' to '"never" if read-only; "on-request" if workspace-write or danger-full-access'

expectations.text: The output HANDBOOK.md does not present 'on-failure' as the current/default approval-policy value (historical references OK, but not as the operative default)
expectations.evidence: 0 unqualified 'on-failure' matches. Note: the original handbook never contained the literal string 'on-failure' — it said 'coupled to sandbox'. The agent correctly updated the vague phrase to concrete values.

expectations.text: Major section headings from the original handbook are preserved — targeted update, not a full rewrite. At least 80% of original headings present.
expectations.evidence: 15 level-2 (##) headings present. Agent confirms: 'No other sections were modified. The /delegate runbook has no approval-policy flag so it was unaffected.'

expectations.text: HANDBOOK.md exists in outputs/ and has at least 1000 characters of content
expectations.evidence: File exists at without_skill/outputs/HANDBOOK.md with 27,615 characters

expectations.text: The handbook has an Overview or introduction section that describes the system's purpose
expectations.evidence: Has '## 1. What This System Does' as the first section — no literal 'Overview' heading but functionally equivalent introduction section

expectations.text: The handbook mentions both execute_scout and process_turn (the two main MCP tool entrypoints)
expectations.evidence: grep counts: execute_scout=3 mentions, process_turn=4 mentions. Both appear in the two-call protocol section

expectations.text: The handbook contains at least one table with symptom/cause/recovery columns (or equivalent failure mode documentation)
expectations.evidence: 4 matches for failure mode keywords. Agent summary lists '8. Failure modes' as a dedicated section covering restart behavior, git ls-files failure, missing rg, checkpoint overflow, conversation limit, store eviction

expectations.text: The handbook includes a numbered verification or smoke test procedure an operator can actually run
expectations.evidence: 11 matches for verification/verify keywords. Agent summary lists '10. Verification procedure — concrete checks an operator can run to confirm correctness'

expectations.text: audit_report.md exists in outputs/ and has at least 500 characters of content
expectations.evidence: File exists with 10,426 characters

expectations.text: Report contains all four standard categories: Accuracy Issues, Coverage Gaps, Currency Concerns, and Structural Issues (or equivalent headings)
expectations.evidence: Report uses severity-based structure (High/Medium/Low) with a single '## Findings' table, followed by '## What Is Accurate'. Zero matches for 'accuracy' or 'coverage' as section headings. The 4-category format (Accuracy/Coverage/Currency/Structure) is absent.

expectations.text: Report explicitly identifies the approval-policy default change (on-failure to on-request) as an accuracy issue or currency concern
expectations.evidence: Finding #1 (High severity): 'Stale approval-policy default — after commit fa1531b, the workspace-write default changed from on-failure to on-request.' 7 keyword matches in total.

expectations.text: The report is findings-only — it describes issues rather than applying fixes. The report does not claim to have modified the handbook.
expectations.evidence: Report lists findings in a table format with Severity/Type/Location/Commit columns. No claim of modifying the original file.

expectations.text: Report ends with or contains a Recommended Priority or priority ranking section
expectations.evidence: Report ends with '## What Is Accurate' section listing correct elements. No 'Recommended Priority' section or equivalent ranking. Items have severity (High/Medium/Low) but no explicit prioritization rationale.

expectations.text: HANDBOOK.md exists in outputs/ and has substantial content (at least 5000 characters)
expectations.evidence: File exists with 45,358 characters — full handbook content preserved

expectations.text: The output HANDBOOK.md contains 'on-request' as the approval-policy default value
expectations.evidence: 1 match for 'on-request'. Agent updated the table from 'coupled to sandbox' to '"never" if read-only; "on-request" if workspace-write or danger-full-access'

expectations.text: The output HANDBOOK.md does not present 'on-failure' as the current/default approval-policy value (historical references OK, but not as the operative default)
expectations.evidence: 0 unqualified 'on-failure' matches. The original handbook never contained this string literally. Agent searched and found no 'on-failure' strings, updated 'coupled to sandbox' to explicit values.

expectations.text: Major section headings from the original handbook are preserved — targeted update, not a full rewrite. At least 80% of original headings present.
expectations.evidence: 15 level-2 (##) headings present — identical count to with_skill. Only the inputs table in /codex runbook was changed.
